source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Recognise and show, using diagrams, families of common equivalent fractions; count up and down in hundredths; add and subtract fractions with the same denominator.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This review-stage sample focuses on equivalent fraction reasoning and same-denominator addition/subtraction before moving into hundredths retrieval.

objective.child_goal: I can spot equivalent fractions and add or subtract fractions with the same denominator.
objective.statement: Use visual models to identify common equivalent fractions, count in hundredths, and add or subtract fractions with the same denominator within one whole.
misconceptions: Thinks a larger denominator always means a larger fraction.
Adds denominators when adding fractions with the same denominator.
Compares shaded pieces without checking whether the wholes are the same size.
Counts hundredths as tenths or skips the zero placeholder in decimal links.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Fractions that look different but show the same amount.
vocabulary.audio_script: Equivalent fractions have different names but the same value.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two equal-length fraction bars shaded to the same endpoint.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The bottom number. It shows how many equal parts make the whole.
vocabulary.audio_script: The denominator tells us the number of equal parts in one whole.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A whole split into equal slices with the bottom number glowing.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The top number. It shows how many parts we have.
vocabulary.audio_script: The numerator tells us how many equal parts are counted.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Shaded parts lift upward and count into the top number.

vocabulary.child_explanation: One of one hundred equal parts.
vocabulary.audio_script: A hundredth is one tiny part when a whole is split into one hundred equal parts.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A 10 by 10 hundred-square with one cell highlighted.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Check: are these fraction bars the same-size whole?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Secure fair comparison before equivalent fraction reasoning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Before we compare fractions, the wholes must be the same size.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two fraction bars resize until their whole lengths match.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Slide the fraction bars until the shaded endpoints match.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce equivalent fractions as equal amounts with different partitions.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: One half and two quarters reach the same point, so they are equivalent.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Stacked bars for 1/2, 2/4 and 4/8 align with a glowing endpoint.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Three eighths plus two eighths makes five eighths.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model adding numerators while the same denominator names the equal parts.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The parts are still eighths, so we count three eighths and two more eighths.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Eighths tiles snap into one bar while the denominator stays fixed.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Connect counting in hundredths to a 10 by 10 area model.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Fill three full rows and seven more squares to show thirty-seven hundredths.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A hundred-square fills by tens then ones.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which is larger: one third or one sixth?
teaching_sequence.audio_script: If one whole is split into more equal parts, each part is smaller.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A cake split into thirds and sixths, then one piece from each is compared.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Rex says 2/5 + 1/5 = 3/10. Teach Rex what stayed the same.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation to repair adding-denominators errors.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I added the bottom numbers too. Can you show me why the pieces are still fifths?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion changes fifths into tenths, then the learner locks the denominator.

manipulatives.purpose: Make equivalence, same-size wholes and same-denominator operations visible through bars, tiles and hundred-squares.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners align fraction bars, lock same denominators, build sums with tiles and count hundredths on a 10 by 10 grid.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard bar alignment, non-colour pattern fills, audio labels for numerator and denominator, and reduced-motion static comparisons.

manipulatives.purpose: Build hundredths as counted parts of one whole and connect rows of ten to tenths without using fraction bars.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners reveal map tiles in complete rows and single squares, name the shaded fraction in hundredths and regroup ten hundredths as one tenth to unlock a route marker.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard row, column and direct-count controls select squares without precise pointing; single-switch scanning offers add ten, add one, remove and check; static mode provides a numbered 10 by 10 grid plus numeric entry; reduced-motion mode fills squares instantly with patterns, outlines and spoken row-and-square totals.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave equivalence, comparison and same-denominator operations only after learners consistently check same-size wholes.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Check whether fraction comparisons use the same-size whole.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two visual wholes + same/different size decision + repair prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep visual differences obvious early; no trick perspective effects.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Match common equivalent fractions such as 1/2, 2/4, 3/6 and 4/8 using aligned bars.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: target fraction + three candidate bars + endpoint alignment
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use Year 4 common families before unfamiliar denominators.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Add and subtract fractions with the same denominator by counting equal parts.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: fraction tile expression + build bar + numerator answer
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep totals within one whole for sample pack scope.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Count up and down in hundredths using 10 by 10 grids and number-line jumps.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: hundred-square fill or number-line jump + fraction notation response
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Review decimal links separately before release content.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain equivalence, denominator size and same-denominator operation reasoning through spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short mixed item from prior blueprints + misconception distractor
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use quick, readable items for warm-up quests.

body.prompt: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Look for a bar that reaches the same endpoint.
Two quarters cover the same amount as one half.
question_variants.explanation: 2/4 is equivalent to 1/2 because both show half of the same-size whole.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "2/4". 2/4 is equivalent to 1/2 because both show half of the same-size whole.
feedback.repair: Look for a bar that reaches the same endpoint. Two quarters cover the same amount as one half. Use that clue to repair surface shading match, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this fraction bar match, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "2/4": 2/4 is equivalent to 1/2 because both show half of the same-size whole.
feedback.misconception_check: surface shading match
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: The pieces are all fifths.
Count the numerators: 2 + 1 = 3.
question_variants.explanation: The denominator stays 5 because the parts are still fifths. 2 fifths plus 1 fifth is 3 fifths.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "3/5". The denominator stays 5 because the parts are still fifths. 2 fifths plus 1 fifth is 3 fifths.
feedback.repair: The pieces are all fifths. Count the numerators: 2 + 1 = 3. Use that clue to repair adds denominators, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this same denominator build, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "3/5": The denominator stays 5 because the parts are still fifths. 2 fifths plus 1 fifth is 3 fifths.
feedback.misconception_check: adds denominators
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Which fraction is larger when the wholes are the same size?
choices: They are equal
Cannot tell
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: Compare one piece from each whole.
Sixths are smaller pieces than thirds.
question_variants.explanation: 1/3 is larger because splitting a whole into 3 equal parts makes bigger pieces than splitting it into 6 equal parts.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "1/3". 1/3 is larger because splitting a whole into 3 equal parts makes bigger pieces than splitting it into 6 equal parts.
feedback.repair: Compare one piece from each whole. Sixths are smaller pieces than thirds. Use that clue to repair larger denominator larger fraction, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this tap choice, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "1/3": 1/3 is larger because splitting a whole into 3 equal parts makes bigger pieces than splitting it into 6 equal parts.
feedback.misconception_check: larger denominator larger fraction
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: A hundred-square has 37 small squares shaded. What fraction is shaded?
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: The whole has 100 equal squares.
37 shaded squares means 37 hundredths.
question_variants.explanation: 37 out of 100 equal parts is 37/100. The expected response is 37/100. 37 shaded squares means 37 hundredths. The whole has 100 equal squares.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "37/100". 37 out of 100 equal parts is 37/100.
feedback.repair: The whole has 100 equal squares. 37 shaded squares means 37 hundredths. Use that clue to repair tenths hundredths confusion, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this hundred square count, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "37/100": 37 out of 100 equal parts is 37/100.
feedback.misconception_check: tenths hundredths confusion
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-scribed responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

hints: The pieces are eighths before and after subtracting.
7 eighths take away 3 eighths leaves 4 eighths.
question_variants.explanation: Subtract the numerators and keep the denominator: 7/8 - 3/8 = 4/8.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "4/8". Subtract the numerators and keep the denominator: 7/8 - 3/8 = 4/8.
feedback.repair: The pieces are eighths before and after subtracting. 7 eighths take away 3 eighths leaves 4 eighths. Use that clue to repair subtracts denominators, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this same denominator build, the number, equation or representation must show the same quantity and relationship. The mathematical evidence supports value: "4/8": Subtract the numerators and keep the denominator: 7/8 - 3/8 = 4/8.
feedback.misconception_check: subtracts denominators
feedback.support_message: Use the concrete or visual model and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to scribe the chosen number or step. Fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Bar A shows 1/2 of a 12-unit whole and bar B shows 1/3 of a 12-unit whole. Is it fair to compare their shaded fractions yet?
choices: No fractions can be shown with bars
Yes, the bars represent same-size wholes
No, equal wholes can never be compared
Yes, denominator size is all that matters
body.manipulative_route: Use same-whole bars, counters or a raised 0-to-1 number line with stepper controls.

body.prompt: Which model gives a fair visual comparison of 3/4 and 5/8?

Two bars with unequal parts

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks a larger denominator always means a larger fraction.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner chooses 1/6 as larger than 1/3 because 6 is greater than 3.
repair_sequence: Split one into thirds and one into sixths.
Lift one piece from each and compare the piece sizes.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner chooses the larger unit fraction and explains that more equal parts make smaller pieces.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Adds denominators when adding fractions with the same denominator.
repair_sequence: Lock the denominator label as fifths.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner gives a same-denominator sum and says what the denominator represents.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Compares shaded pieces without checking whether the wholes are the same size.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner matches fractions from differently sized bars or shapes based on shaded area alone.
repair_sequence: Repeat the comparison with equal wholes.
Ask learner to reject an unfair comparison.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner checks same-size wholes before comparing or matching.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm fraction bars with no flashing slice effects.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use static aligned bars and instant tile add/remove states.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read each fraction as 'numerator over denominator' and as counted parts, such as 'three fifths'.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use labelled numerator, denominator, whole, equal parts and hundredths cards.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Present one decision at a time: same whole, equal parts, then answer.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow replay, bar alignment, tile recount and denominator-lock reminders before submitting.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Frame mistakes as expedition clues and reward clear explanations, not only final answers.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach whole, equal, part, shaded, numerator, denominator, equivalent and hundredth with icons.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires matching common equivalent fractions and adding/subtracting same-denominator fractions within one whole; secure requires explaining denominator meaning and counting hundredths accurately across review items.
evidence.parent_summary: Can use fraction pictures to spot equal fractions and add or subtract fractions with the same denominator.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Checks same-size wholes, identifies common equivalent fractions, keeps denominators fixed for same-denominator operations and counts hundredths on a 10 by 10 model.
next_actions: Teacher review of generated equivalents before pilot.
Add decimal hundredths links after vocabulary review.
Interleave with measure and area models once fraction-bar reasoning is secure.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 4 fractions pack with a deterministic 260-item pilot bank and five preserved curated variants. The bank covers same-whole calibration, common equivalence, tenths and hundredths, same-denominator addition and subtraction, fractions of quantities, number lines, visual models, comparison and misconception reasoning. Generated candidates include strategic untimed mission progression, SEND and dyslexia scaffolds, manipulatives, non-drag supported interactions and evidence-led feedback. Independent mathematics, teacher, accessibility, safeguarding and renderer review remain required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
